diagrams.title: Binary Tree Example for Traversals
diagrams.description: A sample binary tree with 6 nodes to demonstrate all traversal orders
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different traversal outputs: Preorder=A-B-D-E-C-F, Inorder=D-B-E-A-C-F, Postorder=D-E-B-F-C-A, Level=A-B-C-D-E-F

diagrams.title: Recursive Call Flow for Inorder
diagrams.description: Shows how recursive calls explore the tree and when nodes are visited
elements: Call stack building as we go left
Each recursive call pushes and pops from stack
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how recursion and backtracking work in tree traversal

diagrams.title: Level Order Using Queue
diagrams.description: Demonstrates queue operations at each step of level order traversal
elements: Step 1: Enqueue A, Process A, Enqueue B,C
Step 2: Dequeue B, Process B, Enqueue D,E
Step 3: Dequeue C, Process C, Enqueue F
diagrams.purpose: Understanding FIFO behavior and how level-by-level processing works
